content.problemStatement:
TaskGiven an array A and an integer k. Print the Kth Largest element from the array.

A Heap has already been implemented for your convenience.


Input Format

The first line of input contains two integers NNN and KKK, denoting the number of elements in the array and the integer K.
Next line contains NNN integers of the array.


Output Format
In a single line output the Kth Largest element in the array
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and K, the size of the array and the required order. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the Kth largest element of the given array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array sorted in descending order is [134,93,78,48,29,19,17,13,2,1]. The 4th largest element is 48.
Sorted descending: [5,4,3,2,1]. The 2nd largest element is 4.

solution.approach: Maintain a min‑heap (priority queue) of size at most K while scanning the array. Insert each element into the heap; if the heap size exceeds K, remove the smallest element. After processing all numbers, the heap root holds the Kth largest value. This works because the heap always keeps the K largest elements seen so far, and the smallest among them is exactly the Kth largest overall. The algorithm runs in O(N log K) time per test case and uses O(K) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log K) per test case, where N is the array size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(K) auxiliary space for the heap.
